questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The correct order of the stability of the following compounds based on hyperconjugation is
questions.solution:
Hyperconjugation increases the stability of alkene which does not have any other resonance effect. And hyperconjugation effect depends on the number of alpha hydrogen around the alkene. More the number of alpha hydrogen atoms more stable the alkene will be.

So, stability order will be IV>III>II>I.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.q: In Lassaigne's extract of organic compound both nitrogen and sulphur are present, which gives blood-red colour with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Fe</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> due to the formation of
questions.solution:
When the organic sample containing nitrogen and sulphur is strongly heated with sodium metal the ionic compound sodium thiocyanate is formed. Sodium thiocyanate gives blood-red colour with neutral FeCl3.

Na +N+C+S→NaSCNSCN-  + Fe3+→FeSCN2+

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following does not show geometrical isomerism?
questions.solution:
For a compound to show the geometrical isomerism, it should fulfill the following conditions

1. Bonds should have restricted rotation

2. The groups/substituents which are bonded to restricted atoms must be different

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: The order of basicity among the following compounds is
questions.solution:
I>IV>II>III

(I) Conjugated acid has three equivalent canonical forms (3 lone pair of electrons on each N-atom).

(IV) Conjugated acid has two equivalent canonical forms (2 lone pair of  electrons on each N- atom).

(II) (2 lone pair of electrons on each N-atom, not in resonance).

(III) Conjugated acid has two equivalent canonical forms (2 lone pair of electrons on each N-atom, but in resonance).
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: The correct order of acidity of the following is
questions.solution: The electron withdrawing groups increases the acidic strength of phenols and the electron releasing groups decreases the acidic strength of phenols. The methyl group and methoxy groups are electron releasing groups at para position. Hence, both p-cresol and p-methoxy phenol are less acidic than phenol. Methoxy group is electron releasing group by +M effect and methyl group is hyperconjugation. Hence, p-methoxy pheniol is less acidic than p-cresol. The correct acidic strength order is

questions.solution: Styrene does not exhibit geometrical isomerism as it has three identical groups (H-atoms) on the doubly-bonded carbon atoms.

questions.q: Which of the following compound(s) has ' $Z$ ' configuration?
questions.solution:
When the groups with higher priority (ie, with high atomic number) are present on same side of double bond, then the configuration is $Z$ but when present on opposite side of double bond, the configuration is $E$.
(Priority : $\mathrm{Br}>\mathrm{Cl}$ and $\mathrm{CH}_3>\mathrm{H}$ ) Hence, compound (i) and (iii) have $(Z)$ configuration.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Which compound amongst the following is not an aromatic compound?
questions.solution:
German chemist and physicist Erich Hückel proposed a theory to help determine if a planar ring molecule would have aromatic properties. His rule states that if a cyclic, planar molecule has 4n+2 π electrons, it is considered aromatic. This rule would come to be known as Hückel's Rule

   

In the above molecule,There is an sp3 hybridized carbon in the ring.Hence, it is not involved in conjugation. As delocalization of π electrons is not there throughout the ring.It is not an aromatic species.

It does not follow Huckel's rule.

But in the other options the species given that is ,andare aromatic as they obey Huckel's rule
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Among the following four compounds
A. Phenol
B. Methyl phenol
C. meta-nitrophenol
D. para nitrophenol
the acidity order is
questions.solution:
An electron withdrawing group (-I showing group like $\left.-\mathrm{NO}_2,-\mathrm{CN}\right)$ stabilises the phenoxide ion, thus when present, increases the acidity of phenol. On the other hand electron releasing groups ($+I$ showing group like $-\mathrm{CH}_3,-\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{H}_5$), when present, decrease the acidity of phenol by destabilising phenoxide ion. Hence, the correct order of acidity of given compounds is \(\underset{(D)}{\text{p-nitrophenol}} > \underset{(C)}{\text{m-nitrophenol}} > \underset{(A)}{\text{phenol}} > \underset{(B)}{\text{methyl phenol}}\)
(due to intermolecular H -bonding)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
Decreasing order of stability of given carbocations is?


(1)                  (2)    CH 2 = CH - C ⊕ H 2


(3)    C 6 H 5 - C ⊕ H 2                             (4)    CH 3 - C ⊕ H - CH 3
questions.solution:
The factors affecting carbanion stability:

1. If carbanion is on more percentage s character carbon, it will be more stable.

2. If carbanion is attached to any electron withdrawing group (groups those show negative inductive, mesomeric and hyperconjugation effect), its stability increases.

3. If carbanion is busy in aromatic stability, it will still be more stable than any other factor.

Preference of effects: 

Aromaticity > resonance > mesomeric > hyperconjugation > inductive

(1)         Aromatic    

(2)    CH 2 = CH - C ⊕ H 2 (2 Resonating Structure )

                                                


(3)       C ⊕ H 2  more delocalised (4 Resonating Structure )               

  (4)    CH 3 - C ⊕ H - CH 3 (6 hyper conjugation )
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: To which process aniline and water can be separated by
questions.solution: Continuous extraction is a separation process which consists in distilling water together with other volatile components. The continuous extraction process works on the principle that when a mixture of two or more undissolved liquids are heated, while ensuring that the surfaces of both liquids are in contact with the atmosphere, the vapor pressure exerted by the system increases. This is because it now becomes the sum of the vapor pressures of all of the components of the mixture combined together. This allows for evaporation of elements with high boiling points at much lower temperatures merely by allowing them to form a mixture with water. Aniline is separated from a mixture by using this method.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An aromatic compound of formula <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>7</mn></msub><mi>Cl</mi></math> has how many isomers?
questions.solution:
C7H7Cl has 4 isomers.

In aryl halides, the position of chlorine group is changed to give ortho, meta and para form and the last isomer is alkyl halide.

In the 4th isomer, the main chain is an aliphatic part and phenyl is treated as a side chain. So, this is a type of alkyl halide.
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: $0.16 \mathrm{~g}$ of an organic compound containing sulphur produces $0.233 \mathrm{~g}$ of $\mathrm{BaSO}_4$. Percentage of sulphur in the compound is
questions.solution:
Percentage of S
$\begin{aligned}
& =\frac{32 \times \text { weight of } \mathrm{BaSO}_4 \times 100}{233 \times \text { weight of compound }} \\
& =\frac{32 \times 0.233 \times 100}{233 \times 0.16} \\
& =20
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
